---
title: "Lecture NN: <Lecture Title>"
subtitle: "<Optional Subtitle>"
date: YYYY-MM-DD
week: NN
topics:
  - "<Topic 1>"
  - "<Topic 2>"
  - "<Topic 3>"
authors:
  - name: "Prof. Dr. Andre Guettler"
    affiliation: "Institute of Strategic Management and Finance, Ulm University"
format:
  revealjs:
    output-file: slides.html
  pdf:
    output-file: slides-handout.pdf
  html:
    output-file: notes.html
---

## Learning objectives {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-note title="By the end of this lecture you will be able to"}
- Objective 1
- Objective 2
- Objective 3
:::

::: notes
Speaker note: open with the framing question. Spend ~2 min on context before jumping into the first topic.
:::

# <Topic 1> {.section-divider}

## <Topic 1 — first idea>

- Bullet 1
- Bullet 2
- Bullet 3

::: notes
Talk through bullet 2 in detail; the other two are recap.
:::

## <Topic 1 — definition or formula>

::: {.callout-tip title="Definition"}
A **<term>** is …
:::

$$
\text{Equation} = \frac{\partial f}{\partial x}
$$

# <Topic 2> {.section-divider}

## <Topic 2 — example>

| Column A | Column B | Column C |
|---|---|---|
| value | value | value |
| value | value | value |

## <Topic 2 — illustration>

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[Idea] --> B{Decision}
  B -->|yes| C[Outcome 1]
  B -->|no|  D[Outcome 2]
```

# <Topic 3> {.section-divider}

## <Topic 3 — discussion>

::: {.callout-important title="Open question"}
What is the empirical evidence for …?
:::

# Wrap-up {.section-divider}

## Key takeaways

::: {.callout-tip title="Remember"}
1. Takeaway 1
2. Takeaway 2
3. Takeaway 3
:::

## References & further reading

- Author (Year). *Title*. Journal, Vol(Issue), pp.
- Author (Year). *Title*. Publisher.

## Next lecture

> **Lecture NN+1:** <Title> — <one-line preview>

::: notes
Reminder to flag any pending assignment deadlines.
:::
